adversarial.notes: Independently re-derived (not trusted from generator or Mode-B evidence). Production Proven' line deleted, all nine other levels and both no-promotion-beyond-evidence phrasings intact) correctly FAILs entry 8, naming exactly 'Production Proven'; (b) an opposite-meaning commercial-exclusion sentence ('MUST NOT be embedded' replaced with 'MAY be freely embedded') correctly FAILs entry 10; (c) the real doc with the literal 'evidence hierarchy' phrase kept but all four '- **Tier N' bullet lines deleted correctly FAILs entry 7 (on 'Tier 2', since decoy 'Tier 1-4' range prose survives elsewhere in the section, but no individual 'Tier 2' token does), confirming entries 7/8/10 discriminate on the REAL shipped content, not merely a pre-implementation synthetic fixture. A sanity copy of the unmutated real document was independently re-confirmed to pass entries 3-11 identically in the same miniroot before any mutation was applied. (2) `.agent/evidence/035/diff.patch` was byte-diffed against a freshly captured `git diff HEAD -- ecosystem/README.md` -- zero output, exact match; the patch's one README hunk (03 row Planned->Shipped) is the only tracked-file change, and ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md is correctly new/untracked (expected git semantics, independently verified by entries 1-11 instead). (3) All 22 evidence files' first lines were programmatically compared (json.load, never retyped) against the live contract's own 22 command strings: zero mismatches. (4) A live, second, later re-run of the full 22-entry suite (see checks_passed/root-cause note below) surfaced a genuine, reproducible, root-caused finding: entry 16 (the .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard) now fails, caused solely by feature 037's own concurrently-drafted .agent/contracts/037.json (status 'proposed', name 'Baseline Schema + Fixtures') appearing as a new untracked file after this review's first clean run -- confirmed via three repeated live re-runs of entry 16 alone, all three deterministically failing and naming the identical single offending path, i.e. not flaky (a stable fact of current repo state, not a transient race).

observations: INITIAL LIVE RE-RUN (all 22 commands extracted programmatically from .agent/contracts/035.json via json.load -- never retyped -- executed from the repository root, HEAD d87d88f, deliverable ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md present, ecosystem/README.md's 03 row 'Shipped'): 22/22 PASS, exactly matching Mode B's reported B1-B8-all-PASS result and the generator's own captured evidence bundle. Every one of the 22 .agent/evidence/035-verify-NN.txt files was independently spot-checked (invocation-first line, literal EXIT:0 last line, per methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention) and each first line was programmatically compared byte-for-byte against the live contract's own 22 command strings: zero mismatches.

.agent/evidence/035/diff.patch BYTE-MATCHES a freshly captured `git diff HEAD -- ecosystem/README.md`: `diff -u` between the patch's ecosystem/README.md hunk and the live diff produces zero output. The patch's `git status`-style trailer (`M ecosystem/README.md`, `?? ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md`) also matches the live tree exactly at the time of capture.

REAL-DOCUMENT KILL-SHOT RE-VERIFICATION (per the coordinator's explicit instruction, this Loop-1 review's own three specified mutations re-run against the REAL shipped document, not the Loop-1 pre-implementation synthetic fixture): a 9-of-10 maturity-level scratch mutation of the real doc correctly fails entry 8 (missing exactly 'Production Proven'); an opposite-meaning commercial-exclusion sentence scratch mutation correctly fails entry 10; a tiers-stripped-but-'evidence hierarchy'-phrase-retained scratch mutation correctly fails entry 7. All three probes ran inside a scratch miniroot (real, unmodified tools/verify_lib.sh + schema/frontmatter.schema.json copied in) against a copy of the real ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md; the real repository was never touched by any of the three probes.

FEATURE 035 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA IN .agent/feature_list_005.json CROSS-CHECKED WITH EVIDENCE BY PATH: 'Audit uses evidence hierarchy and maturity model' -> .agent/evidence/035-verify-07.txt + 035-verify-08.txt (EXIT:0 each); 'Commercial readiness is not embedded in generic engineering scoring' -> .agent/evidence/035-verify-10.txt (EXIT:0); the embedded literal whole-file python3 check ('maturity'/'evidence'/'confidence' all lowercase-present) -> .agent/evidence/035-verify-05.txt (EXIT:0), independently re-executed directly against the real file in this review and reconfirmed True/True/True; 'Frontmatter validates against schema/frontmatter.schema.json' -> .agent/evidence/035-verify-03.txt (EXIT:0); 'Compliance validator stays green' -> .agent/evidence/035-verify-14.txt (EXIT:0), independently reconfirmed by directly re-running `bash tools/validate.sh` in this review (SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed). All five acceptance tests are satisfied with evidence traceable by path; no missing acceptance coverage found.

[AMENDED] RECONCILIATION FLAG FOR THE ORCHESTRATOR (feature_list_005.json): feature 035's entry in .agent/feature_list_005.json still reads status='pending' as of this QA pass -- this evaluator did NOT modify feature_list_005.json (out of scope per this review's explicit instruction and the orchestrator-owns-status-flips precedent already applied in .agent/qa/031.json and .agent/qa/034.json). The orchestrator should flip feature 035's status (e.g. to 'complete'/'passed_qa') on the strength of this qa_pass=true verdict.

[AMENDED] RECONCILIATION FLAG FOR THE ORCHESTRATOR (run_state.json): .agent/run_state.json's current_feature still reads '038' (status 'feature_complete') as of this QA pass -- this evaluator did NOT modify run_state.json (out of scope per this review's explicit instruction; run_state.json is orchestrator-owned and batched centrally, per contract 035's own design_notes.run_state_and_immutable_artifacts). The orchestrator should advance current_feature/current_phase bookkeeping to reflect feature 035's now-passed QA.

[AMENDED] LIVE CONCURRENCY FINDING, ROOT-CAUSED, NOT ATTRIBUTABLE TO FEATURE 035'S IMPLEMENTATION -- FLAGGED FOR ORCHESTRATOR RECONCILIATION, NOT A REQUIRED FIX: a SECOND full 22-entry re-run, performed later in this same review session (after the clean 22/22 result above and after the adversarial real-document kill-shot probes, which used only scratch copies), found verification entry 16 (the .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard) now FAILING live, naming the sole offending path `?? .agent/contracts/037.json`. Root-caused with certainty: feature 037 (Baseline Schema + Fixtures, PR-F2, handover F-007) began drafting its own .agent/contracts/037.json concurrently during this review -- confirmed by reading that file directly (feature_id '037', status 'proposed'), which this evaluator did not create, modify, or touch, per the explicit 'do not touch any 036/037 surface' instruction. Contract 035's own entry-16 allow-list was authored (at Loop-1 contract-review time) against the then-known concurrent siblings 036 and 038 only; 037 had not yet begun contract-drafting at that time and design_notes.concurrency_fence explicitly says 037 is 'NOT tolerated here'. Re-ran entry 16 three consecutive times: all three deterministically FAIL, naming the identical single path -- this is a stable fact of current repo state (037's contract is durably present, not a transient race), not a flaky test. This is judged NOT a defect in ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md or the README flip (both independently re-verified sound and unaffected by this finding via entries 1-15 and 17-22, all of which still PASS) -- it is a live demonstration of the known, orchestrator-level, shared-mutable-working-tree hazard where one feature's fixed-at-authoring-time allow-list cannot anticipate a sibling beginning contract-drafting later in the same session. Recommended reconciliation (for the orchestrator, not this evaluator, to action): (a) serialize feature 037's contract landing to occur only after feature 035's QA pass fully closes and the orchestrator's own state has advanced, matching the precedent already used to serialize README-row edits across PR-F2 siblings, and/or (b) re-run entry 16 once more, isolated, at the actual moment of finalizing/merging feature 035, since the concurrent-artifact landscape may again have changed by then. This finding does not change the assessment that feature 035's own deliverable is complete, correct, and fully evidenced -- it is reported for transparency (AH-3) and is the reason checks_passed is reported as 21/22 rather than 22/22 at the moment this verdict was written, even though the feature's own implementation re-runs 22/22 clean whenever no unrelated sibling contract is mid-landing in .agent/contracts/.

SCOPE / NON-GOAL VERIFICATION UNAFFECTED BY THE ENTRY-16 FINDING: entries 15 (scope guard), 17 (dependency-order guard), 18 (schema/tools byte-identical), 19 (skill.json/SKILL.md), 20 (no forbidden ecosystem/*.md), 21 (01/02/CHANGELOG byte-identical), 22 (planning docs byte-identical) were all independently re-confirmed PASS in this same final live re-run -- the concurrency interference is isolated to entry 16's .agent/contracts+.agent/qa pathspec alone, not a broader scope violation.

Repository tree left exactly as found by this QA pass except for this verdict's own new artifact: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after this review shows the identical changed/untracked-path set (including features 036's and 037's own genuine, untouched, concurrently-authored artifacts), now additionally including this .agent/qa/035.json file. No 036 or 037 surface was created, modified, or touched by this QA pass; all fixture/mutation probing (the real-document kill-shots) occurred exclusively in a scratch miniroot under the evaluator's own scratchpad, never at any real repository path.

final_verdict.summary: PASS. Feature 035's deliverable (ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md, plus the ecosystem/README.md one-line 03-row flip) is complete, correct, and fully evidenced. An initial live re-run of all 22 contract verification commands, independently extracted and executed from the repository root at HEAD d87d88f, produced 22/22 PASS, exactly matching Mode B's reported result and the generator's own evidence bundle (all 22 flat 035-verify-NN.txt files format-compliant and verbatim-matching the live contract's commands; .agent/evidence/035/diff.patch byte-matches a freshly captured git diff HEAD). The three Loop-1 kill-shot mutations (9-of-10 maturity levels, opposite-meaning commercial-exclusion sentence, tiers-missing-but-phrase-present) were re-run against disposable scratch copies of the REAL shipped document and all three correctly discriminate (entries 8, 10, 7 respectively fail on the real content, exactly as they failed on the pre-implementation synthetic fixture). Feature 035's five acceptance tests in .agent/feature_list_005.json are all satisfied with evidence traceable by path, and bash tools/validate.sh independently reconfirms SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed. A second, later re-run of the full suite in this same session surfaced one live, root-caused, non-attributable finding: entry 16 (the .agent/qa + .agent/contracts immutability guard) now fails solely because feature 037 began drafting its own .agent/contracts/037.json concurrently during this review -- a sibling artifact this evaluator did not create or touch, outside feature 035's own scope, and outside contract 035's entry-16 allow-list (authored before 037 began drafting). This is judged an orchestration-level, shared-working-tree concurrency timing issue, not a defect in feature 035's implementation; all other 21 entries, including every other scope/non-goal/immutability guard, remain PASS. checks_passed is reported as 21/22 to reflect this true, current, final live state honestly; qa_pass and final_verdict.approved are nonetheless true because the deliverable itself is sound and the one failing entry is definitively root-caused to an out-of-scope concurrent sibling, not to feature 035. Two [AMENDED] reconciliation flags are raised for the orchestrator: feature_list_005.json's feature-035 status is still 'pending' and .agent/run_state.json's current_feature is still '038' -- neither was modified by this QA pass per its explicit orchestrator-owned-bookkeeping instruction. A third flag recommends the orchestrator re-run entry 16 in isolation at the actual moment of finalizing/merging feature 035, and/or serialize feature 037's contract landing until after feature 035's state has advanced, mirroring the README-row serialization precedent already used across PR-F2 siblings. No issues, no missing acceptance coverage, no unsupported claims, and no required fixes are raised against feature 035's own scope. Neither .agent/run_state.json, feature_list_005.json, nor any 036/037 surface was touched by this QA pass; all probing was scratch-only.
